properties.remark: THUNDERSTORM WINDS RIPPED OFF THE ROOF OF A PORCH AT A HOME ON OPAL VALLEY ROAD ABOUT 3 MILES EAST OF IDABEL.
A PINE TREE WAS BLOWN ONTO A HOME ON COUNTY ROAD 2 IN THE GUERNSEY COMMUNITY WEST OF HOPE. A TREE WAS ALSO BLOWN OVER ONTO POWER LINES NEAR THIS SAME LOCATION.
REPORT FROM MPING: HOMES/BUILDINGS COMPLETELY DESTROYED.
QUARTER-SIZE HAIL REPORTED AT I-30 AND KINGS HWY.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED MAXIMUM WINDS NEAR 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN A HEAVILY WOODED AREA WEST OF OLD ATLANTA ROAD JUST NORTHWEST OF VIVIAN, WHERE IS SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES AS IT CROSSED OLD ATLANTA ROAD. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST NORTHEAST ACROSS A HEAVILY WOODED AREA SNAPPING AND UPROOTING ADDITIONAL TREES BEFORE RIPPING PORTIONS OF A METAL ROOF OFF OF A STORAGE SHED, FLIPPING A TRAMPOLINE ONTO ITS SIDE, AND PUSHING THE SKIRTING INWARD ON A MOBILE HOME ON CANE ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY BEFORE CROSSING PINE STREET/HIGHWAY 1 ON THE NORTH SIDE OF VIVIAN. THE ESTIMATED TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS 1.13 MILES, WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 165 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG EDMONDS CREEK AND MOVED NORTHEASTWARD, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING HUNDREDS OF TREES ALONG ITS 2.7 MILE PATH. SEVERAL HOMES, AWNINGS, AND VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED BY THE FALLING TREES. THE PATH ENDED JUST NORTH OF HWY 2, WEST OF FARMERVILLE. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 95 MPH, AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS 766 YARDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT.
EMERGENCY MANAGER FOR UNION PARISH CONFIRMED AT LEAST A SHORT TREE DAMAGE PATH JUST WEST OF FARMERVILLE, CROSSING HIGHWAY 2. AN NWS STORM SURVEY WILL FINALIZE DAMAGE MAGNITUDE AT A LATER DATE.

*** 1 INJ *** UPDATED TO INCLUDE 1 INJURY. AN EF2 TORNADO BEGAN JUST WEST OF MOUNT ENTERPRISE, TRACKED THROUGH TOWN, AND ENDED 3 MILES NNW OF GARY CITY. ONE HOME HAD MOST OF ITS ROOF REMOVED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US-84 AND W 5TH STREET IN MOUNT ENTERPRISE. EST. PEAK WINDS WERE 115 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 22.61 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS.

AN EF2 TORNADO BEGAN JUST WEST OF MOUNT ENTERPRISE, TRACKED THROUGH TOWN, AND ENDED 3 MILES NNW OF GARY CITY. ONE HOME HAD MOST OF ITS ROOF REMOVED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF US-84 AND W 5TH STREET IN MOUNT ENTERPRISE. EST. PEAK WINDS WERE 115 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 21.28 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS.

THE SOUTH SIDE OF A CARPORT WAS LIFTED AND BENT NORTHWARD AND FOLDED OVER ON TOP OF A STEEL AWNING ON LEOPARD DRIVE IN THE LIBERTY EYLAU COMMUNITY. A GREENHOUSE WAS ALSO DESTROYED. A LARGE LIMB WAS BROKEN OFF OF A NEARBY TREE.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN ACROSS FM 999. ROAD IMPASSABLE.
GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED IN SE CARTHAGE.

*** 1 FATAL, 1 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO BEGAN NEAR US-59 SOUTH OF CARTHAGE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AS THE TORNADO PARALLELED FM-2517. ONE HOME HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED AND ANOTHER WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION. A PAN ANCHORED MANUFACTURED HOME WAS ALSO LIFTED FREE FROM ITS ANCHORAGE AND DESTROYED. A CONTINUOUS SWATH OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED ALL ALONG THE PATH. A LARGE PINE TREE FELL ON A SINGLE-WIDE MANUFACTURED HOME KILLING ONE PERSON AND INJURING ANOTHER. EST. PEAK WINDS WERE 125 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 16.68 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 1600 YARDS.

*** 1 FATAL, 3 INJ *** UPDATED TO INCREASE INJURY COUNT TO 3. AN EF2 TORNADO BEGAN NEAR US-59 SOUTH OF CARTHAGE AND TRACKED NORTHEAST. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED AS THE TORNADO PARALLELED FM-2517. ONE HOME HAD ITS ROOF REMOVED AND ANOTHER WAS SHIFTED OFF ITS FOUNDATION. A PAN ANCHORED MANUFACTURED HOME WAS ALSO LIFTED FREE FROM ITS ANCHORAGE AND DESTROYED. A CONTINUOUS SWATH OF LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED ALL ALONG THE PATH. A LARGE PINE TREE FELL ON A SINGLE-WIDE MANUFACTURED HOME KILLING ONE PERSON AND INJURING ANOTHER. EST. PEAK WINDS WERE 125 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 16.68 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 1600 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO BEGAN IN THE NORTH DESOTO ESTATES SUBDIVISION NEAR STONEWALL WERE A COUPLE OF HOUSES SUSTAINED PARTIAL ROOF LOSS. MOST OF THE DAMAGE AS SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS TO A AN ATTACHED GARAGED OF A HOME WHERE THE EXTERIOR BRICK WALLS SIMPLY MORTARED TO THE FOUNDATION COLLAPSED AFTER FAILURE OF THE GARAGE DOOR LED TO THE ROOF BEING REMOVED. EST. PEAK WINDS WERE 105 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 12.06 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 675 YARDS.

AN EF1 TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF LA HWY 2 AND LES PARKER RD NEAR THE NOLAN PLACE SUBDIVISION. MOST OF THE DAMAGE CONSISTED OF LARGE BROKEN TREE BRANCHES AND SPORADIC UPROOTS AND SNAPS OF TREES, BUT A SMALL METAL OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.17 MILES. MAX. WIDTH WAS 300 YARDS. EST. PEAK WIND SPEED WAS 105 MPH.

A BRIEF TORNADO SNAPPED A FEW TREES ALONG FAIRVIEW POINT RD. AND CONTINUED ALONG A 0.9 MILE PATH, LIFTING A SECTION OF ROOFING MATERIAL ON A MOBILE HOME AND SNAPPING LARGE TREE LIMBS NEAR THE PINE COVE MARINA BEFORE LIFTING. THE PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 50 YDS AT ITS WIDEST POINT, AND MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 100 MPH.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HWY 1 NEAR THE WESTDALE COMMUNITY, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES ALONG ITS 13.6 MILE TRACK, BEFORE LIFTING ALONG US HWY 371 JUST NORTH OF WOODARDVILLE. THE TRACK WIDTH WAS 300 YDS AT ITS WIDEST PONIT, AND MAXIMUM WINDS WERE 105 MPH.

A STRONG SQUALL LINE WITH AN EMBEDDED SUPERCELL PRODUCED A BROAD SWATH OF WIND DAMAGE STARTING NEAR FOREMAN AR AND ENDING NEAR ASHDOWN AR. DAMAGE WAS MAINLY LIMITED TO SNAPPED AND UPROOTED TREES LAYING IN A SOUTHEASTERLY DIRECTION, ALTHOUGH A FEW STRUCTURES DID EXHIBIT MINOR ROOF DAMAGE FROM THE WIND, WHILE MANY OTHERS WERE DIRECTLY DAMAGED BY FALLING TREES. THE SURVEYED PATH LENGTH WAS AROUND 17 MILES, MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH AROUND 8 MILES, AND ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS RANGED FROM 70 TO 90 MPH.
WIDESPREAD TREES DOWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE COUNTY.

A BRIEF EF1 TORNADO CROSSED OVER LITTLE RIVER 21 AND THE INTERSECTION OF LITTLE RIVER 15 AND LITTLE RIVER 713, DOING CONCENTRATED CONVERGENT TREE DAMAGE IN THE MIDST OF MUCH WIDER STRAIGHT LINE WIND DAMAGE THROUGHOUT THE SOUTHERN PORTION OF LITTLE RIVER COUNTY. DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES IN THE TORNADO PATH WAS VERY MINIMAL. MAX WIND SPEED WAS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH, MAX WIDTH OF 150 YARDS, WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 2.4 MILES.

THE TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN AS CAPTURED VIA STORM CHASER DRONE FOOTAGE. THE MOSTLY HORIZONTAL FUNNEL IS SEEN VISIBLY LOFTING GROUND MOISTURE INTO THE AIR. NO DAMAGE WAS FOUND AND THUS AN EF-UNKNOWN DAMAGE RATING IS ASSIGNED. A BEST ESTIMATE IS THAT THE TORNADO WAS INTERMITTENTLY ON THE GROUND FOR 1.17 MILES.

A SHORT-LIVED EF-0 TORNADO EMBEDDED WITHIN A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS QUICKLY TOUCHED DOWN ALONG HIGHWAY 783 AND DOWNED SEVERAL SMALL AND LARGE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD LIMBS. THE TORNADO PRODUCED VERY SPORADIC DAMAGE TO TREE LIMBS AND THEN LIFTED AS IT CROSSED U.S. HIGHWAY 371. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED UP TO 70 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.18 MILES WITH A MAX WIDTH OF 40 YARDS.
